reviews.review_msg: Goes way too fast during examples in class. Takes a lot about things that don't even relate to the class (like his ex-gfs). Cannot study for exam. You can do and understand all the questions assigned from textbook (like I did) but have no clue how to do half the questions on the final. Average for final was under 40%.

Bernie's classes have been some of the memorable because he puts a lot of effort into making tedious material enjoyable. He's a great person over all, and he will give you all of the help you need if you just ask him. Definitely my favorite professor that I've had at SFU. Sad to see him leave!

i kind like this prof. he is  like a real person, not just a prof far away out there. very outgoing.  but the course material is super hard. hver, if you email him, he will immediately reply. studying through emails is a way to pass this course. i studied a lot for the midterm 2, found out the course material is actual quite fun. enjoy the process.

He kept going on and on about his personal stories. I took two classes with him and guess what he was always behind the schedules that he gave out at the beginning of the semester. The way he teaches is fine but because he interrupts the lecture with his stories so most of what I got out of the courses are his personal stories, not the materials
Arrogant, opinionated, and defensively hostile when questioned.  I wish I had taken 207 from someone else.

Bernie is a great prof who will do everything to help you get through the course. As long as you do the practice questions and listen during lecture you'll be fine. He lets you pick the weight of your assignments on your grade. He does throw pop quizes but only a few during the semester. Fair tests and no final!

Class was boring but Bernie tried to make it as fun as possible. Basically, if you came to lecture and did the practice questions (and actually paid attention in lecture) you were guaranteed a high mark. He did catch a couple people cheating and he did end up giving them zeros (which is fair) and honestly, he gave some wisdom applicable to life.
Useless yet mandatory lectures; final based solely on book material. Avoid.

Awful prof ever! Never teaches anything, and let you have an exam based on whatever you read from book. Even through he gives the "suggested questions", it helps nothing on the exam. Awful! I would never ever get his course again!
Terrible prof. Tells stories all  class and does not teach course material. Then hard final afterwards?

Took 3 other classes with him. He goes on with his life stories a lot, but a lot of times, they connect the course material to real life. You will definitely have to do readings and learning by yourself to succeed in the class. Bernie is open for help  if you go for it.

Attending his class is so fun. He keeps students entertaining with his stories. I like his method of teaching. The grade does not concentrate on the midterm and the final. He divides the grade into many activities involved individual and team efforts. That are actually very nice and helpful. He is def one of my fav teacher at SFU.

Took BUEC 232 with him, really enjoyed it so I decided to take BUS 336 with him as well. The class itself was really hard, but Bernie made it somewhat bearable. I really like his teaching style, and you can see that he makes an effort to make sure that his students understands everything to the best of their abilities. Highly Recommended.
Great prof.  Lots of entertainment value in the lectures.

Super cool prof! Probably one of the best class ive ever take  at sfu. Super useful. I saved all his slides and files because it can be applied to many things in the future! Really awesome class and prof if you are serious about starting a business

This guy is amazing. He somehow made me actually enjoy regression... His classes are entertaining and the examples are very helpful to supplement the readings. Attend class, do the readings and practice questions and it's an easy A.
Great prof, really cares about his students. Willing to go way out of his way to help students and has probably the fairest tests out of any class I've taken.

He flies through lectures so you must preread. However, he keeps the mood of the class light. He is extremely nice and goes out of his way to help you if you need it. Best professor i've had. His exams test based off the learning curve so do lots of practice. I'd love to take another class with him.
best prof ever, super sarcastic, but very helpful, very fair, tries his best to avoid cheating in his pop quizzes, will do attendance so better show up for class

Thoroughly enjoyed his class, and teaching style; sure he does tell a lot of stories, but he does talk about the content when necessary. If you need help he is more than willing to explain stuff, great class and great prof! excited to take more classes with him!

Only thing good about this guy is that he has interesting stories. Other than that he is an awful prof. Lectures are useless because he doesn`t POST THE NOTES and he skims through slides only to talk about some of his stories. Exams are tough and require you to work quickly. I will never take this prof again. Just a bad experience.

Tends to fly through lecture slides very quickly, so do pre-readings! Goes over sample problems thoroughly. Will spend time doing lecture-irrelevant things, such as stories and things that should be done during office hours. Holds many review sessions. Exams must be done at a relatively fast pace. You'll have a love-hate relationship with Bernie.
Best Prof I have ever had. Very successful entrepreneur.
Nice guy, terrible prof. Forced you to attend his useless lecture because of his quizzes. Avoid at all costs.

Not a very good teacher. He tends to move through the lecture notes very quickly, but forces you to attend because he has random quizzes. Despite his lack of teaching abilities he's definitely a nice, very intelligent guy; he's just not cut out for teaching, wouldn't recommend.
Best prof ever.
Terrible lecturer, he's a cool dude but he can't teach. Flies through the slides and expects that you learn from the book. Do not recommend!!

He's a great person. His teaching style is very different from other instructors. You'll learn not only the course material, but valuable life lessons. The course itself is challenging and he has high expectations. Bernie is very experienced in the field of operations.

One of the best business profs i've ever had. Laid back but covers important topics. He's very enthusiastic about the topic and has very interesting class exercises. If you're uptight, this might not be the class for you (which seems why some people gave him bad reviews).

Fair, helpful, pragmatic, and incredibly entertaining. He is someone you will look up to, and someone you will work hard not to disappoint. Bernie has nothing but the most genuine care for his students, so be warned: you WILL fall in love.
This prof keeps giving a 4hr exam in a 2hr period, and nobody in the class has ever finished any of his final exams in time.

:D No regret to choose him to study. However, his assignments are extremely difficult. Be prepared to do each in 2 days if he says "2 hours" xD. Otherwise, no complaints at all. His requirement for each group is really fun.

One of the hardest course I have ever take. He talks a lot of "funny stories" but does not really focus on the lecture. Too much of work for a 4 unit course. You will have individual test, team test, assignments(extremely hard,especially when the TA are useless), fair MT... You have to spend more than 4 hours per week otherwise you will FAIL!!!

Bernie is a great prof who motivates his students to do their best. He does have high expectations, but if you study hard and work hard he rewards you. He does NOT play favorites as you submit your assignments anonymously which he later correlates to your student ID. One of my favorite profs at SFU, definitely the most knowledgeable.
So far, one of the best professor I have ever had at sfu. He is a Funny guy, and very helpful. attend each class and study for quizes for A+.I didnt go to class but studied and ended up with a B.

Holds review sessions before exams and genuinely cares for his students. Keeps a good balance between learning and fun. As much as smaller class sizes would be helpful I found he did a wonderful job teaching the material by doing actual problems. Assignments, quizzes, and exams are fair but still tough so keep up. Definitely recommended.

By far the most interesting Prof i have ever had, he is very funny. Distributes candies and others things in class. He is very helpful too, a but busy guy because he teaches 4-5 classes. Take pop quizes so attendance is mandatory. If u are a very student he is a very guy to take course with, he helps a lot.

Spends most of the two hour class telling stories about his glory days - however has surprise pop quizzes so attendance is mandatory. Has a bit of an attitude and gets overwhelmed by his workload and responds by refusing to answer emails, etc. Nice guy, not a great prof for this course, probably smaller classes.

By far the best professor I have ever had. Very helpful. The class is hard, but it is stats after all. He makes it as simple as possible. You still have to work for your grade, but if you do, it shows. An upstanding guy and an incredible asset to have at SFU. If you can take any class with him, do it!
Stand up guy, who can appreciate the struggles of a student.  Class act.
Great guy, hes awesome and best prof ever. Take this course with him. You will not be disappointed. - Vincent
Very easy class if you make the effort to study. Online assignments are super easy, overall great prof! Also very funny!

Bernie is a great teacher who actually cares to learn his students names and their goals.  He genuinely wants to help his students' careers too. Expect to work hard and make sure you have a good group to work with because expectations are high and he'll call you out if you didn't put in enough effort. Overall one of my favourite profs at SFU!
One of the best professors at SFU. Take a class with him!

Overall a great professor; one of the few that has relevant real-world knowledge and work experience. BUS 477 is a challenging but rewarding class. Expect to learn how to actually apply your knowledge. Quizzes are important but don't bother to read the textbook, just look at the slides. A fair bit of work, but well worth it.

Incredibly smart professor, don't be deterred by his stories. He does have high expectations but I think this is one of the most useful classes. He is also very personable. One of my fave profs for sure. Quizzes are extremely important if you want a good mark - mostly from his slides.

He does seem to play favourites, but i wasn't one of them for sure and still got an A. he gave me like 6/10 for participation and and i talked every class haha. anyways, mostly true from the other comments but OK overall

Vague Guideline. Plays Favourites. Immature. Arrogant. That about sums up who kind of professor he is. Be aware that unless you can make him like you, you can never get a good grade. Classes are also useless because he is unprepared and he only talks about his irrelevant stories.

I agree with the previous person. Vague & lengthy outlines. Expectations are extremely high, but doesn't teach anything useful. Just rambles on about his stories and blonde wife. EI could be improved.
takes favourites, unless your one of his favourites don't expect an A grade. marking scheme i terrible, he doesn't seem to have one and super vague but extremely wordy outlines.
